Cats sleep about 70 percent of their lives.
A group of cats is called a clowder.
Cats whiskers are roughly as wide as their body for spatial sensing.
House cats share about 95.6 percent of their DNA with tigers.
Cats can jump roughly six times their length in one leap.
Most cats are lactose intolerant as adults.
A cat nose print is as unique as a human fingerprint.
Cats have an extra organ that helps them taste scents in the air.
The oldest known pet cat was found in a Cyprus grave around 9500 years ago.
Cats conserve energy by sleeping more than many mammals.
Some cats can run nearly 30 miles per hour in short bursts.
Cats use slow blinking to signal trust to people and other cats.
A cat spine can rotate more flexibly than many animals, aiding landing on feet.
Cats meow far more at humans than at other adult cats.
Free-ranging cats often have larger territories than many expect.
